title: Phegopteris stenophylla
taxonomy: Phegopteris stenophylla belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as the polypody ferns. The genus Phegopteris is a part of this family and is characterized by its leafy, fern-like appearance. Phegopteris stenophylla is a species of fern within the genus Phegopteris. The classification of Phegopteris stenophylla is further divided into the class Polypodiopsida, which is a subclass of the division Pteridophyta. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this species.

intro: Phegopteris stenophylla, also known as the Narrow-leaved Maidenhair or Chinese maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various conditions such as arthritis, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
